sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.

Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: All 9 grid blocks are green but level not complete. Clicking the blue square didn't change anything. The arrow keys might control navigation or a cursor on the board.
- Goal model: Click all 4 white blocks in the bottom-right grid to turn them green, matching the cross pattern shown in the bottom-left panel.
- Action model: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT for navigation, MOUSE for clicking. Need to test what they do.
- Recent findings: Grid is fully green. The top track blue indicator has been shrinking throughout the game.
- Open questions: What's the next task? Maybe click the remaining gray blocks (corners and center) that don't have purple dots?
- Plan: Try arrow keys to see if they control something on the board.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 16 of about 36 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after pressing RIGHT. Let me look at what changed - comparing step 15 to step 16. Looking at the images, the small blue dot on the right side of the gray bar seems to have moved slightly. Let me check the segmentation to see exactly what changed.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: RIGHT key moved the blue dot on the top track. The arrow keys control the position of the blue indicator on the gray bar.
Plan: Check what changed and figure out what the blue dot's position means.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

def find_node_by_color(seg, color):
    return [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == color]

print("Before - blue nodes:")
for n in find_node_by_color(before, 'b'):
    min_r = min(r for r,c in n['boundary'])
    min_c = min(c for r,c in n['boundary'])
    print(f"  color={n['color']}, bbox=[{min_r},{min_c}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("After - blue nodes:")
for n in find_node_by_color(after, 'b'):
    min_r = min(r for r,c in n['boundary'])
    min_c = min(c for r,c in n['boundary'])
    print(f"  color={n['color']}, bbox=[{min_r},{min_c}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  color=b, bbox=[19,42], pixels=2

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blue dot moved from column 39 to column 42 (3 pixels to the right). So RIGHT moves the blue dot right along the gray bar. Let me see how far the bar extends and try to move it all the way to the end, or see if there's a target position.
Looking at the gray bar (node 5): bbox=[19,17]-[22,42], so it extends from col 17 to col 42. The blue dot was at col 39 and moved to col 42, which is the right edge of the bar.
Now the blue dot is at the far right. Maybe I need to click something or press another key now. Let me try clicking the blue dot or see if the level advances.
Actually, wait - maybe the goal is to move the blue dot to match some target position. Let me look at the overall game more carefully. The top track has:
- A blue square on the left (at col 12-16)
- A gray bar extending from col 17 to col 42
- A blue dot that moves along the bar
Maybe the blue dot represents a "slider" and I need to set it to a specific position. But the grid is already all green...
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game works in phases:
1. Click blocks to make them green (done)
2. Move the slider to a target position
Or maybe I need to move the slider to trigger something. Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if it goes further, or try LEFT to go back.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: RIGHT moves the blue dot on the top track rightward. The dot moved from col 39 to col 42 (the right edge of the gray bar). The gray bar extends from col 17 to col 42.
Plan: Try pressing LEFT to move the dot back and see if there's a specific target position.

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
